data.description:
## Context
Currently ticket-link.sh only accepts `blocks`, `depends_on`, and `relates_to` as valid relation values (line 418). `duplicates` and `supersedes` need to be added. They are non-blocking semantic markers (like `relates_to`) with directional semantics:
- `duplicates`: source is the duplicate, target is the canonical ticket
- `supersedes`: source is the new/replacing ticket, target is the superseded/obsolete ticket
Neither is bidirectional (unlike `relates_to`).

## What to do
Create `tests/scripts/test-ticket-link-duplicates-supersedes.sh`.

## Tests to write

### test_link_duplicates_succeeds
- Create two tickets A and B
- Run `ticket link A B duplicates`
- Assert exit code 0
- Assert a LINK event file exists in A's ticket directory
- Assert `ticket show A` output contains `B` in deps with relation `duplicates`

### test_link_supersedes_succeeds
- Create two tickets A and B
- Run `ticket link A B supersedes`
- Assert exit code 0
- Assert `ticket show A` output contains `B` in deps with relation `supersedes`

### test_link_duplicates_is_not_bidirectional
- Create tickets A and B
- Run `ticket link A B duplicates`
- Assert B's ticket directory does NOT have a LINK event pointing to A (not bidirectional, unlike relates_to)

### test_link_supersedes_is_not_bidirectional
- Same as above with supersedes

### test_link_duplicates_roundtrip_through_unlink
- Create A, B; link A -> B with duplicates
- Unlink A -> B
- Assert the link is no longer active (LINK is cancelled by UNLINK in event replay)
- Assert `ticket show A` does NOT show B in deps

### test_link_invalid_relation_still_rejected
- Run `ticket link A B invalidrelation`
- Assert exit code non-zero (guard still works for unknown relations)

## Files to create
- `tests/scripts/test-ticket-link-duplicates-supersedes.sh` — new RED test file

## Acceptance criteria
- All 6 tests fail RED before T2 is applied (ticket link will reject "duplicates"/"supersedes" as invalid)
- Tests use standard helpers
- Script is executable

## Testing mode
RED — defining behavioral contract for new relation values
data.title: Write RED tests for duplicates and supersedes link relations
